models.description: Two-stage stochastic integer program for integrated backup rolling-stock allocation (first stage) and timetable rescheduling on a space-time network (second stage) under disruptive events with uncertain, time-variant passenger demand. First-stage binary decisions place backup trains at stations; per-scenario second-stage decisions select space-time train arcs (the rescheduled timetable) and route passenger flows so as to minimize expected weighted passenger waiting/travel cost plus investment cost. Canonical (extensive-form) extraction taken from the time-dependent multi-scenario builder time_dependent_solver_space_time_backup_integer_multi_scenario_new(); the same formulation also appears as a non-time-dependent extensive form, as single-scenario subproblems, and inside Benders / L-shaped decomposition (see extraction_notes).

sets_indices.name: T
sets_indices.meaning: discrete time stamps (time horizon), indexed t = 0..T_stamp-1

sets_indices.name: W
sets_indices.meaning: set of stochastic scenarios w (demand realizations)

sets_indices.name: V
sets_indices.meaning: space-time network nodes n = i*T_stamp + t (one per station-time pair)

sets_indices.name: A
sets_indices.meaning: space-time arcs: travel arcs (station i to i+1, time +t_running/t_interval) and waiting arcs (same station, time +1); index 0 is a reserved dummy arc fixed to 0

sets_indices.name: A_trav
sets_indices.meaning: subset of A that are travel (running) arcs, used for train-capacity constraints

sets_indices.name: D_orig
sets_indices.meaning: depot/origin nodes where existing trains and candidate backup trains may enter (train_esist_position = {0,2,5,6,8,10,14,16,18,22})

sets_indices.name: D_dest
sets_indices.meaning: destination nodes: space-time nodes at the last station, i.e. n >= (station-1)*T_stamp

parameters.name: train_exist
parameters.meaning: number of existing in-service trains entering the network

parameters.name: T_stamp
parameters.meaning: number of discrete time stamps in the horizon

parameters.name: h_min
parameters.meaning: minimum headway (number of time stamps) between two consecutive trains entering a travel arc

parameters.name: t_running
parameters.meaning: section running time between adjacent stations (time units)

parameters.name: t_interval
parameters.meaning: duration of one time stamp (discretization granularity), used as t_running/t_interval = travel-arc time span

parameters.name: train_capacity
parameters.meaning: passenger capacity of one train on a travel arc

parameters.name: investment_cost
parameters.meaning: fixed cost of allocating one backup train at a station

parameters.name: investment_cost_depot
parameters.meaning: fixed cost of a depot train (used in result accounting / commented objective term)

parameters.name: penlty
parameters.meaning: penalty weight on un-alighted (detained) passengers at the horizon end

parameters.name: w_l
parameters.meaning: weight relating detained-passenger and travel-time objective components (used in result decomposition / commented term)

parameters.name: big_M
parameters.meaning: big-M linking passenger boarding/flow to whether a train serves the incoming travel arc

parameters.name: O_iw
parameters.meaning: total origin (boarding) passenger demand at station i in scenario w

parameters.name: Dst_iw
parameters.meaning: total destination (alighting) passenger demand at station i in scenario w

parameters.name: b_itw
parameters.meaning: time-variant passenger arriving (boarding) volume at station i, time t, scenario w

parameters.name: alpha_itw
parameters.meaning: cumulative alighting-passenger volume at station i up to time t in scenario w (from data files)

parameters.name: train_esist_position
parameters.meaning: fixed station indices at which existing trains (and backup candidates) originate

decision_variables.name: z_i
decision_variables.meaning: first stage: 1 if a backup train is allocated/positioned at station i

decision_variables.name: g_{iw}^t (g_{nw})
decision_variables.meaning: second stage: 1 if the backup train at station i (space-time node n=i*T_stamp+t) is injected into the network at that node in scenario w (dummy-origin entry of backup train)

decision_variables.name: x_{aw}
decision_variables.meaning: second stage: 1 if space-time arc a is selected (a train traverses arc a) in scenario w; defines the rescheduled timetable. Arc index 0 is a dummy fixed to 0

decision_variables.name: f_{aw}
decision_variables.meaning: second stage: passenger flow on space-time arc a in scenario w

decision_variables.name: y_{itw} (alight)
decision_variables.meaning: second stage: number of passengers alighting at station i, time t, scenario w

decision_variables.name: u_{itw} (board)
decision_variables.meaning: passenger boarding variable at station i, time t, scenario w (declared; used implicitly via flow definitions)

objective.expression_latex: \min \; \sum_{i \in I} c\, z_i \;+\; \sum_{w \in W} p_w \Big[ \sum_{i \in I} \rho\, T\, \big(\alpha_{i,T-2,w} - \sum_{t=1}^{T-2} y_{itw}\big) \;+\; \sum_{i \in I}\sum_{t=0}^{T-2} t\, y_{itw} \Big]
objective.expression_plain: minimize investment cost of allocated backup trains (sum_i investment_cost * z_i) plus expected second-stage cost over scenarios: a penalty (penlty * T_stamp) on passengers not alighted by the end of the horizon, plus total weighted passenger travel/wait time (sum over i,t of t * passenger_alighting). Investment term added at cpp:1934-1937; penalty term at cpp:1918; travel-time term at cpp:1923-1932.

constraints.name: backup_train_injection_link
constraints.indexed_over: i in I, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: in each scenario, a backup train is injected into the space-time network at exactly one time at station i iff it was allocated there (links first-stage z to second-stage dummy-origin entry).

constraints.name: dummy_arc_zero
constraints.expression_plain: reserved dummy arc (index 0) carries no train and no passenger flow.

constraints.name: flow_balance_origin_nodes
constraints.indexed_over: n in D_orig (existing/backup origin nodes), w in W
constraints.expression_plain: train conservation at origin/depot nodes: outflow equals inflow plus one existing entering train plus any injected backup train.

constraints.name: flow_balance_transshipment_nodes
constraints.indexed_over: n in V not in D_orig and n < (station-1)*T_stamp, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: train flow conservation at interior space-time nodes (inflow plus any backup injection equals outflow).

constraints.name: destination_train_count
constraints.expression_latex: \sum_{n \,:\, n \geq (station-1)\cdot T_{stamp}} \big(x_{a^{in,trav}_n,w} + x_{a^{in,wait}_n,w}\big) = train\_exist + \sum_{i \in I} z_i
constraints.expression_plain: total train flow reaching destination (last-station) nodes equals existing trains plus number of allocated backup trains.

constraints.name: headway
constraints.indexed_over: i in I, j in {h_min-1..T_stamp-1}, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: minimum-headway constraint: at most one train may enter a station's travel arc within any window of h_min consecutive time stamps.

constraints.name: train_capacity_on_travel_arcs
constraints.indexed_over: a in A_trav, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: passenger flow on a travel arc cannot exceed train capacity, and is zero unless a train traverses the arc.

constraints.name: passenger_flow_origin_injection
constraints.indexed_over: i in I, t=0, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: all origin passengers at station i enter the network at the first time stamp; no alighting at t=0.

constraints.name: passenger_flow_balance_alighting_def
constraints.indexed_over: i in I, t in {1..T_stamp-2}, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: alighting passengers at (i,t,w) equal boarding arrivals plus net passenger flow into the node (inflow minus outflow).

constraints.name: alighting_requires_train_bigM
constraints.indexed_over: i in I, t in {1..T_stamp-2}, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: passengers can only alight at a node if a train serves the incoming travel arc (big-M linking).

constraints.name: cumulative_alighting_demand_cap
constraints.indexed_over: i in I, j in {1..T_stamp-2}, w in W
constraints.expression_plain: cumulative number of passengers alighted at station i up to time j cannot exceed the cumulative alighting demand (data) for that scenario.

instances.name: Output_Passenger_Board.csv
instances.size: 5 instance groups R{10,20,25,30,35}-1440-720 (capacity 1440, T_stamp 720); board files 1.4-4.6 MB each. First row: investment cost, number of time units, instance type; second row: station indices 1..26; following rows: per-scenario blocks of dynamic passenger arrival rates per station per time unit (e.g. rows 4-723 = scenario 0).

instances.name: Output_Passenger_Alight.csv
instances.size: 5 instance groups; alight files 1.8-6.3 MB each; same block layout as board file, each element = cumulative alighting volume alpha_{itw}.

instances.name: Output.csv (results)
instances.size: 5 result files (~4 KB each): fleet size, ATT (seconds), objective value, CPU time, optimality gap for methods NO BRS / Randomly / LSM / ILM (paper Table 11). Not model input.

models.notes: All numeric parameter values are compile-time constants in src/stdafx.h:13-42, not read from data files (only passenger demand b_itw and alpha_itw are loaded). The space-time network (nodes V, arcs A, travel/waiting arc topology) is built procedurally in Space_Time_Network() (cpp:208-240) and the in/out travel/waiting arc lookups in_travel_arc_find/in_waiting_arc_find/out_travel_arc_find/out_waiting_arc_find (cpp:241-294); a travel arc connects station i at time t to station i+1 at time t+t_running/t_interval, a waiting arc connects (i,t) to (i,t+1). The canonical formulation above is the time-dependent multi-scenario extensive form (cpp:1766-2016). The repo contains several other builders of the SAME formulation: (a) solver_space_time_backup_integer_multi_scenario_new (cpp:296-535) - non-time-dependent extensive form using O/Dst totals and a destination-demand cap instead of cumulative-alighting caps, objective penalty weight 1; (b) single-scenario integer subproblem solver_space_time_backup_integer_single_scenario_revised (cpp:539-778); (c) LP relaxation solver_space_time_backup_linear_single_scenario_revised (cpp:780-1010); (d) time-dependent single-scenario LP/MIP variants ..._revised_1 (cpp:1270-1516, continuous arc vars) and ..._revised_2 (cpp:1519-1764, integer arc vars) used as Benders/L-shaped subproblems; (e) the Benders master problem master_problem() (cpp:2018-2102) over first-stage z_i with optimality cuts theta >= sum_x*(Qx - LB) + Qx, fixing z[5]=z[17]=z[20]=0. Decomposition drivers benders_decomposition() (cpp:2104-2188, parallel) and L_METHOD_benders_decomposition() (cpp:2191-2247) and entry _tmain() (cpp:2249-2393, currently calling benders_decomposition()) are algorithmic, not part of the MILP. These are recorded as variants of one formulation rather than separate models[] entries because they share identical decision variables and constraint families. Passenger demand is randomized in stochastic_parameter_setting() (cpp:121-206) via rand()%p_volume_para in addition to the CSV inputs.

extraction_notes: The repository ships NO written mathematical formulation (the README describes only requirements, data layout and how to run); the README points to the paper (doi 10.1287/ijoc.2022.1233) and an ongoing-development repo (JerryYINJIATENG/TARP) for the model. Therefore the math symbols (z_i, x_{aw}, f_{aw}, y_{itw}, g_{nw}, etc.) were ASSIGNED during extraction to map the C++ identifiers; they are NOT taken from in-repo notation, and exact equation numbering from the paper could NOT be byte-verified in-repo. The paper DOI (10.1287/ijoc.2022.1233) and software DOI (10.1287/ijoc.2022.1233.cd) are taken from README.md:9,19,29 - INFERRED to be the correct formulation reference but not independently verified against the paper text. Constraint and objective line refs are all real and point at model.add(...) / expr += ... in time_dependent_solver_space_time_backup_integer_multi_scenario_new(). Several objective/constraint blocks in other functions are commented out (e.g. investment term in the LP relaxation, alternative destination caps); only active code in the canonical function is transcribed. The 'boarding' variable passenger_boarding is declared and bounded but appears unused in active constraints (the time-variant b_itw enters via the parameter passenger_time_board), recorded with low certainty. Network node/arc cardinalities (Space_Time_Arc_Num, space_time_travel_arc) are computed at runtime in _tmain (cpp:2269-2289) and were NOT executed (no run/build per rules), so |A| is left symbolic. Data files reference Beijing metro (per data/README.md). All constants in src/stdafx.h. JSON validated to parse.
